mnemonics: FaaS-T Food: Think of Serverless as 'Functions-as-a-Snack' - a quick, small, and easy-to-digest 'Hello World' instead of a full meal (server).
keyPoints: A 'Hello World' function is the simplest serverless unit, often just a few lines of code.
The cloud provider (AWS Lambda, Azure Functions, etc.) automatically manages the runtime environment and scaling.
You are charged only for the execution time of your function, not for idle server capacity.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to configure the function's trigger (e.g., an HTTP endpoint) to correctly invoke it.
Assuming the function runtime environment has persistent storage; it is typically stateless and ephemeral.
Including large dependencies that exceed the deployment package size limit for the platform.
